perturbation.original_text: Some of th e information in each of these categor ies is confidential in nature or constitutes a trade secret. This information received from a party (including of a ny of the Corporations), either orally or in writing, during the course of the Project shall be deemed to be confidential information (Confidential Information) for purposes of this Confidentiality Ag reement. Notes, documents, summaries or reports which are prepared from Confidential Informa tion to the extent such specifically refer or relate to Confidential Information are them selves Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Some of th e information in each of these categor ies is confidential in nature or constitutes a trade secret. This information received from a party (including of a ny of the Corporations), either orally or in writing, during the course of the Project shall be deemed to be confidential data (Confidential Information) for purposes of this Confidentiality Ag reement. Notes, documents, summaries or reports which are prepared from Confidential Informa tion to the extent such specifically refer or relate to Confidential Information are them selves Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'confidential information' to 'confidential data' in one instance creates ambiguity. While the parenthetical definition remains '(Confidential Information)', the use of 'confidential data' raises questions on whether all 'Confidential Data' is considered 'Confidential Information' under the agreement. This is an in-text contradiction within Section 1, where both phrases are used but not explicitly equated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient may be provided with access to selected information (oral, written, or electronic) of the other (including of any of the Co rporations) which helps meet a regulatory purpose or business need, including, but not limited to, that information whic h relates or refers to: business planning; internal controls; computer, data processing, or communications architectures or systems; electronic data processing architectures, applications, programs, routines, or subroutines; business affairs and methods of operation or proposed methods of operations, techniques or systems of a party (including of any of the Corporations) or any customer of a party (including of the Corporations); financial or other non-public information. Some of th e information in each of these categor ies is confidential in nature or constitutes a trade secret. This information received from a party (including of a ny of the Corporations), either orally or in writing, during the course of the Project shall be deemed to be confidential data (Confidential Information) for purposes of this Confidentiality Ag reement. Notes, documents, summaries or reports which are prepared from Confidential Informa tion to the extent such specifically refer or relate to Confidential Information are them selves Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' and the changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Data' while still including the parenthetical '(Confidential Information)'. The contradicted text includes both 'confidential data (Confidential Information)' and 'Confidential Information'. The change introduces ambiguity, because it isn't clear if 'Confidential Data' is equivalent to 'Confidential Information', thus creating an in-text contradiction as per the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, where the contract 'uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations'.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient acknowledges the sensitive and secret nature of the Confidential Information it will have access to during the term of this Agreement and agrees that it will treat such Confidential Information as strictly confidential and shall exercise the same degree of care in the protection of the Confidential Information as the Recipient exercises with respect to its own proprietary property and trade secrets, but in no event shall it be less than a r easonable degree of care given the nature of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient acknowledges the sensitive and secret nature of the Confidential Information it will have access to during the term of this Agreement and agrees that it will treat such Protected Material as strictly confidential and shall exercise the same degree of care in the protection of the Confidential Information as the Recipient exercises with respect to its own proprietary property and trade secrets, but in no event shall it be less than a r easonable degree of care given the nature of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Material' introduces a new undefined term. It is now unclear if 'Protected Material' falls under the definition of 'Confidential Information' or represents something different. This ambiguity contradicts the intent of Section 2, which is to ensure the recipient treats all 'Confidential Information' with a specific degree of care, and that creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient acknowledges the sensitive and secret nature of the Confidential Information it will have access to during the term of this Agreement and agrees that it will treat such Protected Material as strictly confidential and shall exercise the same degree of care in the protection of the Confidential Information as the Recipient exercises with respect to its own proprietary property and trade secrets, but in no event shall it be less than a r easonable degree of care given the nature of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Material'. The explanation states this creates an undefined term and ambiguity, which contradicts the original intent to ensure consistent treatment of 'Confidential Information'. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because it uses 'Protected Material' without defining it, leading to conflicting interpretations of obligations related to confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall not disclose Confidential In formation to any person or entity, except its employees or partners involved in the Project, or to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than fulfilling its obligations under the Project.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall not disclose Confidential In formation to any person or entity, except its staff or partners involved in the Project, or to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than fulfilling its obligations under the Project.
perturbation.explanation: Substituting 'employees' with 'staff' creates potential confusion. Although they may seem synonymous, 'staff' can sometimes include contractors or other non-employee personnel. This contradicts the clause in Section 3. If 'staff' is interpreted more broadly than 'employees,' it is unclear whether the disclosure restriction applies to contractors or other non-employee personnel and is an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall not disclose Confidential In formation to any person or entity, except its staff or partners involved in the Project, or to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than fulfilling its obligations under the Project. Recipient acknowledges and agrees that breach of this Confidentiality Agreement by Recipient and/or anyone employed by or otherwise associated with Recipient may also constitute a violation of the Inside r Trading Act and/or other securities and other laws. Access to or disclosure of Confidential Information by Recipient shall be limited to those employees or partners covered by a then effective legal obligati on or agreement with Reci pient that prohibits the disclosure or unauthorized use of confidential in formation deemed confidential by Recipient ( Authorized Employees ). The existence of such an agreement with an employee or partner of the Recipient shall not operate to relieve the Recipient fro m liability for acts of such employee or partner that result in a breach of this Confidentiality Agreement. 2
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'employees' with 'staff.' The contradiction explanation states that 'staff' can sometimes include contractors or other non-employee personnel, potentially broadening the scope of who is covered by the confidentiality agreement. The contradicted text limits disclosure to 'employees or partners covered by a then effective legal obligation or agreement', implying a more restrictive definition than the potentially broader 'staff.' Therefore, the change introduces an in-text contradiction because it's unclear if the same disclosure restrictions apply to contractors under the new 'staff' terminology, making the obligations inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: Each copy, including its storage media, shall be marked Confidential, and also in clude all copyright, tradem ark and other proprietary notices which appear on the original . The Recipient agrees that all C onfidential Information, including any copies thereof, shall be returned to NASDAQ OMX or destroyed within ten (10) days of the expiration or other termination of the Project.
perturbation.changed_text: Each copy, including its storage media, shall be marked Confidential, and also in clude all copyright, tradem ark and other proprietary notices which appear on the original . The Recipient agrees that all Private Information, including any copies thereof, shall be returned to NASDAQ OMX or destroyed within ten (10) days of the expiration or other termination of the Project.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Information' creates ambiguity. While the instruction to mark copies as 'Confidential' remains, the return/destruction obligation now applies to 'Private Information.' It's unclear whether all 'Private Information' is considered 'Confidential Information' or vice versa, leading to confusion on which information must be returned or destroyed. This contradiction is in-text, happening within section 4, since is no longer clear wich information to return.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall not make copies of the Confid ential Information except for those copies required for use by Authorized Employees in the performance of the Agreement. Each copy, including its storage media, shall be marked Confidential, and also in clude all copyright, tradem ark and other proprietary notices which appear on the original . The Recipient agrees that all Private Information, including any copies thereof, shall be returned to NASDAQ OMX or destroyed within ten (10) days of the expiration or other termination of the Project.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Information' introduces ambiguity regarding which information the Recipient is obligated to return or destroy. The contradicted text and changed text both exist in the same section, and the modification creates an in-text contradiction because the Recipient is instructed to mark copies as 'Confidential,' but the return obligation applies to 'Private Information'. It's unclear whether all 'Private Information' is 'Confidential Information' leading to a contradiction as it is no longer clear which information needs to be returned, aligning with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient acknowledges that NASDAQ OMX, b ecause of the nature of the Confidential Information, would suffer irreparable harm in the even t of a material breach of the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement in that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate NASDAQ OMX for such a breach, and that in the event of any material breach or threatened material breach by the Recipient of any such provisions, NASDAQ OMX shall be entitled, in addition to such other legal or equitable remedies which might be available, to inju nctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction against the threatened material breach or continua tion of any such material breach without showing or proving any actual damages sustained by NASDAQ OMX.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient acknowledges that NASDAQ OMX, b ecause of the nature of the Confidential Data, would suffer irreparable harm in the even t of a material breach of the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement in that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate NASDAQ OMX for such a breach, and that in the event of any material breach or threatened material breach by the Recipient of any such provisions, NASDAQ OMX shall be entitled, in addition to such other legal or equitable remedies which might be available, to inju nctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction against the threatened material breach or continua tion of any such material breach without showing or proving any actual damages sustained by NASDAQ OMX.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' in Section 9 introduces inconsistency. Throughout the rest of the document, the standard is 'Confidential Information'. The legal remedy of injunctive relief hinges on the nature of 'Confidential Information,' and this change creates uncertainty on whether the court could consider 'Confidential Data' with the same importance as 'Confidential Information,' and this an in-text contradiction, by using two different therms for the same concept throughout the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient acknowledges that NASDAQ OMX, b ecause of the nature of the Confidential Data, would suffer irreparable harm in the even t of a material breach of the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement in that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate NASDAQ OMX for such a breach, and that in the event of any material breach or threatened material breach by the Recipient of any such provisions, NASDAQ OMX shall be entitled, in addition to such other legal or equitable remedies which might be available, to inju nctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction against the threatened material breach or continua tion of any such material breach without showing or proving any actual damages sustained by NASDAQ OMX. If NASDAQ OMX prevails in any action brought to enjoin a material breach or threatened breach of this Confidentiality Agreement, it shall be entitled to reasonable attorney's fees and costs in connection with such legal proceeding. This Confidentiality Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York, without regard to its principles of conflicts of laws.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' introduces an inconsistency. The legal remedy of injunctive relief hinges on the nature of 'Confidential Information,' and this change creates uncertainty on whether the court could consider 'Confidential Data' with the same importance as 'Confidential Information.' The contradicted text uses 'Confidential Data'. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
